hero.title: Gateway to East & Central Africa
hero.subtitle: United for development through exemplary service delivery.

governor.title: A Message from the Governor
governor.name: H.E. Dr. Paul Otuoma
governor.message: It is a great honour to welcome you to the Gateway to East and Central Africa, renowned for its optimal climatic conditions for agriculture and its hospitable citizens. My foremost commitment is to safeguard the autonomy of the great people of Busia County who elected me to office. Together, we are building a county that delivers quality services, drives economic growth, and ensures equitable development for all residents.
governor.fullMessage:
It is a great honour to welcome you to the Gateway to East and Central Africa, renowned for its optimal climatic conditions for agriculture and its hospitable citizens. My foremost commitment is to safeguard the autonomy of the great people of Busia County who elected me to office.

Our administration is guided by four key pillars: Agricultural Transformation, Universal Healthcare, Infrastructure Development, and Human Capital Development. These pillars form the foundation of our County Integrated Development Plan (CIDP) and every programme we implement.

We are committed to transparency and accountability in the management of public resources. Through our open governance approach, we ensure that every shilling allocated to Busia County works for the benefit of our people.

I invite investors, development partners, and all stakeholders to join us in this journey of transformation. Busia County, with its strategic location at the Kenya-Uganda border, offers immense opportunities for trade, agriculture, and investment.

Together, united for development, we shall build a prosperous Busia County that our children will be proud to inherit.

footer.aboutText: The County Government of Busia is committed to driving equitable and sustainable development for all residents through effective service delivery and prudent resource management.
footer.visionText: To foster equitable and sustainable economic development through effective and efficient mobilization and utilization of available resources.
footer.missionText: To provide high quality service to Busia residents through well governed institutions and equitable resource distribution.
footer.address: County Headquarters, Off Busia-Kisumu Road, Busia Town
footer.poBox: P.O. Box Private Bag, 50400, Busia, Kenya
footer.copyright: © 2025 County Government of Busia. All Rights Reserved.

history.aboutContent:
Busia is one of the 47 counties of Kenya, located in the former Western Province. The county headquarters are in Busia town, located along the Busia-Kisumu Road. With a population of 893,681 according to the 2019 Kenya Population and Housing Census, Busia County is a vibrant and diverse region.

The county borders Uganda to the west, Bungoma County to the north, Kakamega County to the east, and Siaya County and Lake Victoria to the south. This strategic positioning makes Busia the Gateway to East and Central Africa, serving as a critical cross-border trade corridor.

Busia County is predominantly inhabited by the Luhya community — including the Abakhayo, Abamarachi, Abasamia, and Abanyala sub-groups — and the Teso community. The county’s rich cultural diversity is one of its greatest strengths.

The main economic activities include agriculture (sugarcane, maize, beans, and cassava), fishing along the shores of Lake Victoria, and cross-border trade through the Busia and Malaba border points. Busia County is a member of the 14-county Lake Region Economic Bloc (LREB), which promotes regional economic cooperation.

executives.intro: The County Executive Committee (CEC) is the cabinet of the county government, responsible for implementing county legislation, managing county affairs, and overseeing county departments. Each CECM heads a specific department and is appointed by the Governor with approval from the County Assembly.

psb.mandateContent: The mandate of the County Public Service Board is set out in Article 235 of the Constitution of Kenya and elaborated in Sections 55 to 77 of the County Governments Act, 2012.
psb.resp1: Establishing and abolishing offices in the county public service
psb.resp2: Appointing persons to hold or act in those offices
psb.resp4: Exercising disciplinary control over and removing persons holding or acting in those offices
psb.resp5: Making recommendations on remuneration, pensions and gratuities to the Salaries and Remuneration Commission
psb.func1: Establish and abolish offices as required
psb.func2: Appoint and confirm persons to county offices
psb.func3: Exercise disciplinary control and remove persons from office
psb.func4: Prepare regular reports for submission to the County Assembly on the execution of its functions
psb.func5: Promote in the county public service the values and principles referred to in Articles 10 and 232 of the Constitution
psb.func6: Evaluate and report to the County Assembly on the extent to which values and principles are complied with in the county public service
psb.func7: Facilitate the development of coherent, integrated human resource planning and budgeting for personnel emoluments
psb.func8: Advise the county government on human resource management and development
psb.func9: Make recommendations to the Salaries and Remuneration Commission on remuneration, pensions and gratuities
psb.achievement1: Successfully recruited 934 staff members through competitive and transparent processes to fill critical positions across county departments
psb.achievement2: Established a comprehensive performance management system for county public servants
psb.achievement3: Developed and implemented human resource policies aligned with the Constitution of Kenya and County Governments Act

privacy.section1Content: When you use our website, we may collect the following information: personal information you provide through our contact form (name, email address, phone number), email addresses submitted through our newsletter subscription form, and anonymous usage data collected through website analytics.
privacy.section2Content: We use the information we collect to respond to your enquiries and feedback, send newsletter updates about county activities, tenders, and vacancies (only if you have subscribed), improve our website and services, and comply with legal obligations.
privacy.section3Content: Contact form submissions are retained for a maximum of 2 years. Newsletter subscriptions are maintained until you choose to unsubscribe. Analytics data is aggregated and anonymized.
privacy.section4Content: Our website uses Google Maps to display our office location. Google Maps may collect data according to Google's Privacy Policy. We do not sell, trade, or otherwise transfer your personally identifiable information to third parties.
privacy.section5Content: You have the right to request access to the personal data we hold about you, request correction of inaccurate data, request deletion of your data, and unsubscribe from our newsletter at any time. To exercise these rights, please contact us at info@busiacounty.go.ke.
privacy.section6Content: For questions about this privacy policy or our data practices, contact: County Government of Busia, ICT Department, P.O. Box Private Bag, 50400, Busia, Kenya. Email: info@busiacounty.go.ke

accessibility.commitmentContent: The County Government of Busia is committed to ensuring that our website is accessible to all people, including those with disabilities. We aim to comply with the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 at Level AA.
accessibility.measure1: All images include descriptive alternative text
accessibility.measure2: All form inputs are properly labelled
accessibility.measure3: The website is navigable using keyboard alone
accessibility.measure4: Colour contrast meets minimum 4.5:1 ratio for body text
accessibility.measure5: The website is compatible with screen readers
accessibility.measure6: A skip-to-content link is provided for keyboard users
accessibility.measure7: The website supports text resizing up to 200%
accessibility.limitationsContent: While we strive for full accessibility, some older documents (PDFs) may not be fully accessible. We are working to remediate these documents over time. If you encounter any accessibility barriers, please contact us.
accessibility.feedbackContent: We welcome your feedback on the accessibility of our website. Please contact us at info@busiacounty.go.ke or call +254 700 106 517 if you encounter any accessibility issues.

deputyGovernor.name: H.E. The Deputy Governor
deputyGovernor.role: Deputy Governor, County Government of Busia
deputyGovernor.about: The Deputy Governor serves as the principal assistant to the Governor in the execution of the functions of the county government. The office plays a critical role in coordinating government programmes and ensuring the smooth running of county affairs.
deputyGovernor.func1: Deputises for the Governor in the execution of county functions
deputyGovernor.func2: Assists the Governor in coordinating county government affairs
deputyGovernor.func3: Acts as the Governor when the Governor is absent or temporarily incapacitated
deputyGovernor.func4: Performs any functions assigned by the Governor or by legislation
deputyGovernor.func5: Serves as a member of the County Executive Committee
